Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed clause; nominal subject + adjectival modifier + subordinate clause
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone is extremely patient and slow to react despite pressure.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: O pam ka o di nyɛla ka o wum
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, family advice, leadership discussions
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects Dagbon cultural praise of patience in chiefs and elders
First Example Sentence: Abdulai kuɣu kpema ka o yuli, hali ka ti yɛli pam pam o pu nyɛ.
Second Example Sentence: N pu bɔri o, kuɣu kpema ka o yuli ka o nyɛ zaaŋ.
Third Example Sentence: Yɛlɛ la daa pam, ka o kuɣu kpema ka o yuli ka o chɛ.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Nominal clause; head noun + adjectival modifier + copular construction
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: Nyɛla kpema ka pam di shɛli
First Example Sentence: Yɛlɛ la nyɛ shɛli ka zugu kpema la di pam.
Second Example Sentence: Ti daa bɔri, ka kpema yɛli ka zugu kpema la di pam.
Third Example Sentence: O pam ka o wum ka zugu kpema la di pam.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase; reflexive imagery with body-part metaphor
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: O be yɛli ka o wum pam pam
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from imagery of scratching one’s head in confusion
First Example Sentence: Fo pu nyiɣisi niŋ zugu la hali zaaŋ.
Second Example Sentence: N daa nyiɣisi niŋ zugu la ka n pu wum.
Third Example Sentence: O be nyiɣisi niŋ zugu la ka o chɛ.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used historically when nobles lost honor
First Example Sentence: Ka o daa yɛli la pam, yuli ka o tiŋa.
Second Example Sentence: Ti wum la ka yuli ka o tiŋa zaaŋ ni.
Third Example Sentence: O bɔri ka yuli ka o tiŋa hali kpema.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Nominal phrase; diminutive noun + evaluative predicate
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common among traders in Tamale markets
First Example Sentence: Fo pu nyɛ biɛla la nyɛ pam.
Second Example Sentence: N daa mali ka biɛla la nyɛ pam.
Third Example Sentence: O yɛli kpema la ka biɛla la nyɛ pam.

Idiom (Dagbani): O ti pam ka o nyɛ
First Example Sentence: Fo ti pam ka fo nyɛ ka fo pu wum la.
Second Example Sentence: O daa ti pam ka o nyɛ hali sukuu ni.
Third Example Sentence: Ti yɛli la ka o ti pam ka o nyɛ.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed nominal clause; noun + copular construction
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in storytelling traditions of Dagbon
First Example Sentence: Ka o daa chɛ la, tiŋa la di zaaŋ.
Second Example Sentence: Ti pam ka tiŋa la di zaaŋ hali kpema.
Third Example Sentence: O yɛli ka tiŋa la di zaaŋ zaaŋ ni.

Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: O yɛli ka o di kpema
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to cultural warnings against pride
First Example Sentence: O yɛli niŋ zugu kpema hali ka kpema wum.
Second Example Sentence: Fo pu yɛli niŋ zugu kpema ni.
Third Example Sentence: O daa yɛli niŋ zugu kpema ka o bɔri.

Idiom (Dagbani): N pu wum ka o yɛli
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative clause; negation particle + verb + complement clause
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: I do not trust what he says.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: N pu sihim o yɛlɛ la
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common everyday expression in Tamale households
First Example Sentence: N pu wum ka o yɛli hali kpema.
Second Example Sentence: Ti pu wum ka o yɛli la zaaŋ ni.
Third Example Sentence: M pu wum ka o yɛli ka o ti pam.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Nominal clause; body-part noun + stative verb
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: The matter is settled or resolved.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used traditionally during mediation by elders
First Example Sentence: Ka ti daa mali la, zugu la gbani.
Second Example Sentence: O yɛli ka zugu la gbani zaaŋ ni.
Third Example Sentence: Ti pam ka zugu la gbani hali kpema.
